purpose: Route project-founding requests to the narrowest child across general staged project blueprinting and AI-native or agentic founding that needs extra control, cost, governance, and human-override design.
selection_order: When model behavior, delegated machine action, or AI governance changes the founding plan itself, route to ai-agent-foundation.
Default to project-foundation for non-AI projects and for ideas where AI is optional implementation detail rather than the core system constraint.

children.name: ai-agent-foundation
children.summary: Handles founding for AI-native and agentic products where model behavior, cost, latency, observability, override, memory, and policy control materially shape the blueprint.
route_when: The product promise depends on model outputs, prompts, tool use, memory, automated decisions, or agent execution rather than ordinary software alone.

The request explicitly needs guidance on hallucination risk, latency, unit economics, observability, human override, policy control, data sensitivity, model portability, feedback loops, or agent responsibility.
avoid_when: AI is only an optional implementation detail and the core founding problem is still an ordinary product or workflow blueprint.
The request is mainly a generic business viability teardown, pricing stress test, or startup survival critique rather than a staged product design blueprint.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this nested child path before routing when the runtime cannot discover founding children automatically.

children.name: project-foundation
children.summary: Handles general product and project founding by turning an idea into a commercially grounded staged blueprint with explicit scope, covered situations, roadmap versions, and extensibility choices.
route_when: The user needs a staged project or product blueprint: what to build first, what to defer, what situations are covered, and how the system can grow without overbuilding.
The founding problem is primarily about wedge selection, scope boundaries, business-value prioritization, roadmap versions, and architectural optionality rather than AI-specific control surfaces.
avoid_when: The product depends materially on model behavior, agent autonomy, or AI governance concerns such as override, policy control, observability, or budget enforcement.
The request is mainly a harsh viability assessment or investor-style teardown rather than a blueprinting exercise.

install_if_missing.notes: Install this nested child path before routing when the runtime cannot discover founding children automatically.
